element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Found after tutorial descent; becomes primary exploration vehicle
Interleaved with tank sections; player exits tank to access confined spaces
Optional tactical upgrades; different variations tested during development
Overlay cosmetic effect; unused pause menu detail suggests gameplay impact was planned
Vine/wire connection mechanic; incompatible with 45-degree cannon aiming, likely cut feature
Unused Spike power-up suggests intended immunity item was cut; penultimate boss context
Core antagonist type; variety escalates through areas
Each area concludes with boss; Area 8 features two bosses in succession
Final confrontation; spike-filled lair suggested by unused asset context

typical_relief_triggers: Exiting tank for on-foot sections in safe enclosed spaces
Boss defeats unlocking progression to new areas
Finding power-up pickups to strengthen weaponry
Safe tank-based travel zones between hazard sections
typical_tension_triggers: Tank damage accumulation without healing items
Transitioning from tank to vulnerable on-foot sections
Gun power depletion during boss fights
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating difficulty curve across 8 areas; alternating tank and on-foot exploration creates rhythm variation; boss-gated progression provides natural tension/relief cycles

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Confined on-foot passages require tank exit; multiple boss room features suggest branching/optional area layouts
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Stage select debug code (Game Genie XTVYGGAV) suggests hidden level/boss access points; prototype had level selector

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial descent introduces tank and basic on-foot movement in safe context; primary mechanics established in Areas 1-2
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Areas 3-6 introduce escalating enemy variety and require Gun Power upgrades; sub-weapon tactics introduced
difficulty_curve.late_game: Areas 7-8 feature spike hazards, sequential boss encounters, and spike-filled boss lairs; final Plutonium Boss represents climactic challenge
notable_spikes: Area 8 transition with spike hazard prevalence (unused Spike immunity power-up indicates difficulty spike recognition)
Two-boss succession in Area 8 (suggests penultimate/final boss separation in original design)
Plutonium Boss fight without immunity item (cut Spike power-up indicates design intention to spike difficulty)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each area contains tank-based overworld exploration, confined on-foot passages requiring tank exit, and culminating boss encounter; progression gated by boss defeat
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Area 1 (establishes pattern), Area 8 (final variation with spike emphasis)

notable_level_patterns.description: Alternating movement between tank-based vulnerability (large but slow) and on-foot agility (small but fragile); spatial design forces player to transition based on passage width/height
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All areas; most pronounced in Areas 4-8

notable_level_patterns.description: Game opens with protagonist descending hole following pet frog (narrative framing); underground cavern aesthetic emphasizes vertical exploration and downward progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Intro sequence sets expectations; reinforced throughout all 8 areas

notable_level_patterns.description: Area 8 bosses occupy rooms with dramatically different features (spike-laden vs. open arena); unused Spike power-up context suggests original design separated these encounters across multiple areas

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - regional differences, unused graphics, level design context, area progression structure)
wikipedia (series overview, Jason/SOPHIA narrative, 8-area structure confirmation)
